_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Retaining walls > 2m require registered building practitioner design and trigger multiple Reg 5.1.5 high-risk categories.

hazards.title: Fall from height > 2m
hazards.risk: Worker fall from wall top during construction or backfilling

controls.title: Where possible, sequence work so workers operate from low side (built ground) not high side (open drop)
Guardrails along open edge ≥ 1m high once height > 2m
Scaffold with toe-board where work above 2m
Fall arrest harness with engineer-approved anchor where edge protection not feasible

hazards.title: Footing trench collapse
hazards.risk: Retaining wall footing > 1.5m deep; trench collapse onto worker

controls.title: Shoring, benching, or battering to safe angle for confirmed soil type

hazards.title: Partial wall collapse during construction
hazards.risk: Newly built wall (unbacked, before drainage/backfill) collapsing if loaded prematurely

controls.title: Wall designed by registered building practitioner; construction strictly to design
No equipment, vehicles, or stockpile above wall until backfill complete and signed off
Engineer hold-point inspections at: footing reo, wall reinforcement, drainage, pre-backfill

hazards.title: Manual handling — sleepers / blocks / stone
hazards.risk: Heavy / awkward materials: sleepers, concrete blocks, stone facing

controls.title: Hiab, skid steer, or excavator with lifting attachment for any unit > 20kg
Two-person lift where mechanical aid not practical and load > 20kg

hazards.title: Mobile plant operating in confined yards
hazards.risk: Skid steer / mini excavator in narrow side access; struck-by

controls.title: 3m exclusion zone; spotter at all times in confined yards
Operator HRWL verified for plant size

hazards.title: Silica dust — block / stone cutting
hazards.risk: Cutting blocks, capping, or stone facing without dust control

controls.title: Wet-cut saw mandatory for blocks, capping, stone

ppe.title: Fall arrest harness (where edge protection not feasible)

tasks: Confirm engineer-stamped design on site; latest revision.
Pre-start toolbox: walk through engineer's drawings, hold-points, sequence with full crew.
BYDA + service location complete; hand-dig within 500mm of services.
Excavate footing per design; shore/batter if > 1.5m.
Set up edge protection BEFORE any work at top of wall reaches 2m AGL.
Reinforcement placement per drawings; engineer inspection HOLD POINT before pour.
Concrete footing pour (see concrete-pour SWMS for additional controls).
Wall construction in lifts per design (block / sleeper / poured); no lift above engineer-specified maximum unbraced height.
Cutting operations: wet-cut only; P3 respirator; cuts done in designated dust-controlled area.
Drainage installation: ag-pipe at base, drainage aggregate behind wall per design.
Engineer inspection HOLD POINT before backfilling.
Progressive backfill in lifts per design; compaction per spec.
No vehicle / heavy load above wall until backfill complete and signed off by engineer.
Wall handover photographs + engineer's final sign-off uploaded to Hub.
